rationale: Contradiction lens, pinned at 41d8e8af in a /tmp clone. The shipped code, ADR-0037, and both SKILL copies are fully coherent on the no-discharge design: zero `refutation_discharged`/re-tally in any .go file (deliberate-removal comments at panel_advisory.go:280-283,498-506), ADR amendments describe satisfy-from-marker only (§6 'simply satisfies the obligation'), SKILL says 'written from a self-contained marker... not by re-reading panel files', plan's AC11 row (plan.md:1194) records DISCHARGE DELETED and every test it names exists. But spec.md was NEVER amended: R2(ii) (line 47) still normatively mandates the satisfy/DISCHARGE/refuse trichotomy with verified vote-state; In Scope (line 56) says reconciliation obtains discharge evidence by 'RE-TALLYING the matched panel... (panel.Tally)'; ADR Touchpoints (line 39) claims ADR-0037 §7 gains 'a refutation_discharged audit' the shipped ADR correctly lacks; AC11(d)/(e) (line 90) require a `refutation_discharged` write the shipped TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll deliberately contradicts (panel_refuted for BOTH X@1 and B@2). consolidated-round-3.md's amendment list ('the plan + ADR-0037 are amended') omits spec.md. The governing requirements artifact thus asserts a deleted mechanism as CURRENT — a live contradiction, docs-only fix.
concrete_changes_required: spec.md: add a dated DESIGN-AMENDMENT note (same precedent as the existing Impacted-Domains amendment note at spec.md:35) recording the operator's 2026-07-10 discharge deletion, explicitly superseding: R2(ii)'s discharge disjunct + its refutation_discharged falsifiers (line 47), the In-Scope reconciliation-re-tally sentence (line 56), the ADR-Touchpoints §7 'or a refutation_discharged audit' and §6 'satisfied or discharged' wording (line 39), and AC11(d)/(e) (line 90) — stating the shipped satisfy-from-self-contained-marker form (panel_refuted written from the marker on every path; AC11(d) settles X@1 AND B@2 as panel_refuted), and pointing at plan.md's Bead-2 DESIGN AMENDMENT + reviews/spec-114-bead2/consolidated-round-3.md as the authoritative record.

plan.md: extend the Bead-2 DESIGN AMENDMENT block (line 379) with one sentence stating that ALL other discharge/re-tally references in the plan are equally superseded — the Decomposition Bead-2 bullet ABOVE it (lines 80-82, not reached by 'described below'), the Bead-3 section's instructions to document 'VERIFIED-discharging (refutation_discharged...)' in ADR §7 (~line 928) and '(or ... a refutation_discharged audit)' in the SKILL (~line 983) which contradict the shipped Bead-3 artifacts, and the ADR-Fitness/Testing-Strategy mentions (~lines 1049-1091, 1128) — since the amendment currently scopes itself to 'this section'.

findings.title: spec.md still mandates the deleted discharge/re-tally mechanism as CURRENT — the only governing artifact never amended after the operator's discharge deletion
findings.detail: The discharge deletion (operator decision, consolidated-round-3.md) was recorded in plan.md (Bead-2 DESIGN AMENDMENT at line 379 + amended AC11 row at line 1194) and shipped correctly in code/ADR/SKILL — but spec.md at 41d8e8af carries no such amendment. Four normative passages assert discharge as current: (1) spec.md:47 R2(b)(ii) 'reconcile ... by exactly one of: satisfy; discharge — ONLY on VERIFIED vote-state ...; or refuse', plus falsifiers naming 'VERIFIED refutation_discharged (moot) audit'; (2) spec.md:56 In Scope: 'the discharge vote-state is obtained by reconciliation RE-TALLYING the matched panel from panelReg.Dir (panel.Tally)' — internal/complete/panel_advisory.go:498-506 states reconciliation does NO re-tally and NO panel read, by design; (3) spec.md:39 ADR Touchpoints claims ADR-0037 §7 gains 'a satisfying panel_refuted or a refutation_discharged audit reconciled BEFORE close' and §6 'satisfied or discharged' — the shipped ADR-0037 amendment contains zero discharge mentions (grep verified), so the spec's description of the ADR amendment contradicts the ADR actually shipped; (4) spec.md:90 AC11(d) requires 'refutation_discharged for X AND panel_refuted for B' and AC11(e) requires verified discharge — the shipped TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll (internal/complete/panel_refuted_test.go:425-499) asserts panel_refuted entries for BOTH X@1 and B@2 and no refutation_discharged key exists anywhere in code, so spec AC11(d)/(e) as written are unsatisfiable by (indeed contradicted by) the shipped implementation. Since the final gate walks AC1-AC12 in spec.md and the spec is the lifecycle's governing requirements artifact merging to main, this is a live doc-asserts-deleted-mechanism contradiction, not marked-superseded history. Docs-only fix; no code change needed.

findings.title: plan.md amendment's supersession scope does not clearly cover discharge text outside the Bead-2 section
findings.detail: The DESIGN AMENDMENT (plan.md:379-380) scopes itself with 'the mechanism described below (steps 5(c)...)' and 'Everything ELSE in this section ... shipped AS WRITTEN' — i.e. the Bead-2 section (lines 377-878). Discharge/re-tally text outside that scope carries no superseded marker: (a) the Decomposition Bead-2 bullet at plan.md:80-82 ('verified-discharges (refutation_discharged, only on affirmative tally evidence...)') sits ABOVE the amendment, unreachable by 'described below'; (b) the Bead-3 section still INSTRUCTS the implementer to write 'VERIFIED-discharging (refutation_discharged, ONLY on affirmative tally evidence...)' into ADR-0037 §7 (~line 928) and '(or, if the RC later resolves naturally, a refutation_discharged audit)' into the SKILL (~line 983) — directly contradicting the shipped Bead-3 artifacts, which correctly contain no discharge; (c) ADR Fitness (~1049-1091) and Testing Strategy (~1128) repeat the discharge design. The amendment's 'ADR-0037 is amended accordingly in Bead 3' hints at supersession but does not state it for these passages. One added sentence broadening the amendment's scope resolves it.

findings.title: Bead tracker records retain discharge-era descriptions (acceptable as history)
findings.detail: .beads/issues.jsonl contains 3 'refutation_discharged' mentions inside bead description text (the Bead-2/Bead-3 briefs as originally planned). These are closed-issue historical records, dated before the redesign, and the authoritative artifacts (plan amendment, consolidated-round-3.md) supersede them; rewriting closed-bead descriptions is not warranted. No change required — recorded for completeness of the contradiction sweep.

findings.title: Positive confirmations for the no-discharge coherence sweep
findings.detail: Verified at 41d8e8af in a /tmp clone: (1) zero 'refutation_discharged'/discharge/re-tally occurrences in any shipped .go file (only deliberate-removal comments, panel_advisory.go:280-283 'deliberately GONE' and :498-506 'No re-tally, no panel read'); (2) shipped ADR-0037 amendments (§1/§3/§6/§7/§8) describe satisfy-from-marker exclusively — §6 'simply satisfies the obligation (the panel_refuted audit is written...)', §7 self-contained refutation_pending marker, no discharge word anywhere; (3) plugins/mindspec/skills/ms-panel-tally/SKILL.md item 5 says the audit 'is written from a self-contained marker persisted at apply time, not by re-reading panel files' — exactly the acceptable rationale form — and diff confirms the .claude copy is byte-identical; (4) plan.md's amended AC11 row (line 1194) matches shipped tests: all 6 spot-checked test names exist (TestPanelRefuted_SatisfyFromMarker_PanelGone, _MarkerWriteFailure_Blocks, _NaturalResolution_NoObligation, _CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll, _HatchStillReconcilesPendingObligation, TestMergeMetadata_FailClosedOnReadError) and the deleted discharge tests are absent; (5) reviews/spec-114-bead2/consolidated-round-3.md durably records the operator decision and rationale. The Bead-2 amendment IS unambiguous for the Bead-2 section's own superseded text.
